title: Terrain system with Perlin noise heightmap
description: Implement src/world/terrain.rs: Generate a 2D heightmap using Perlin noise (4 octaves) from a configurable seed. The heightmap covers the world dimensions (default 1600x1000). Classify each cell into TerrainType based on height thresholds: WATER (<sea_level 0.25), PLAINS (0.0-0.3 above sea level), FOREST (0.3-0.5), HILLS (0.5-0.7), MOUNTAINS (0.7-1.0). Identify COAST cells (WATER adjacent to land). Provide methods: terrain_at(x, y) -> TerrainType, height_at(x, y) -> f32, speed_multiplier(terrain_type) -> f32 (PLAINS 1.0, FOREST 0.6, HILLS 0.4, COAST 0.8, MOUNTAINS/WATER 0.0 impassable), is_passable(x, y) -> bool, is_coastal(x, y) -> bool. Support seasonal speed modifier (winter ×0.7 global). Same seed must produce identical terrain. Create src/world/mod.rs with module declarations.
result_summary:
Everything compiles and all tests pass. Here's what was implemented:

**`src/world/mod.rs`** — Module declaration for `terrain`.

**`src/world/terrain.rs`** — `Terrain` struct with:
- **`new(config)`** — Generates a row-major heightmap using 4-octave fBm Perlin noise from `WorldConfig.terrain_seed`, normalized to [0, 1]. Classifies cells via `TerrainType::from_height()` (already in `types.rs`), then identifies Coast cells (Water adjacent to land) in a second pass.
- **`terrain_at(x, y)`** — Returns the `TerrainType` at a coordinate
- **`height_at(x, y)`** — Returns the raw height value in [0, 1]
- **`speed_multiplier(terrain)`** — Delegates to `TerrainType::speed_multiplier()` (Plains 1.0, Forest 0.6, Hills 0.4, Coast 0.8, Mountains/Water 0.0)
- **`speed_at(x, y, season)`** — Speed with seasonal modifier (Winter ×0.7)
- **`is_passable(x, y)`** / **`is_coastal(x, y)`** — Query helpers
- **Determinism** — Same seed always produces identical terrain
- **6 unit tests** covering determinism, normalization, classification, impassability, winter modifier, and coast adjacency
